data.body:
HANDOFF LOG — MCP-server optional authentication epic (dissimilar-sorcerous-hartebeest): brainstorm → decomposition → plan-review + security-review complete (S7 review pending).

WHAT WAS BUILT: an 8-ticket rebar graph designing OPTIONAL authentication for the rebar MCP server. Epic dissimilar-sorcerous-hartebeest + 7 stories: S1 holy-heartfelt-pheasant (optional Streamable-HTTP transport + hardening + SDK pin + doc-generator fixes), S2 emersed-prettying-eagle (auth framework: composite audience/fail-closed choke point + factory + static verifier + PRM/401 wiring), S3 lost-nonproift-stilt (JWKS/JWT verifier), S4 turbid-isogonal-iguana (RFC 7662 introspection verifier), S5 trogonoid-browny-blowfish (trusted-proxy verifier), S6 witching-subjective-skylark (pluggable custom verifier), S7 fixed-jockeyish-swan (docs + ADR + reference regen). Deps: S2<-S1; S3/S4/S5/S6<-S2; S7<-all.

CONFIRMED DECISIONS (user): (1) scope = optional MCP auth + verify LOCALLY; AWS deploy + Gerrit/CI wiring are follow-on (Gerrit/CI don't consume MCP — ADR-0007 library/CLI in-process). (2) BUILD OWN verifiers on the official mcp SDK TokenVerifier seam; Resource-Server ONLY, no AS/no DCR (delegate identity to org IdP); rejected standalone fastmcp (heavy dep + semver-exempt auth module w/ CVEs). (3) Ship FULL set of 5 verifiers behind one composite: static, JWKS/JWT, introspection, trusted-proxy, custom; PAT(static)+OAuth(JWKS) are the floor. (4) GitHub-user + Gerrit auth OUT of scope (neither is a token issuer). Grounded facts: Claude clients don't REQUIRE DCR (static-header + manual client-id/secret fallbacks), so RS-only is NOT a usability gap — documented in S7.

SECURITY REVIEW (3-reviewer adversarial panel) — blockers folded into the descriptions: (B1) composite is the single audience choke point since SDK never checks .resource (defaults None); every verifier stamps resource=auth_resource_server_url and the composite re-validates it. (B2) trusted-proxy identity is synthesized into an AccessToken that runs through the SAME composite (not around it). (B3) proxy header-spoofing: strip entire X-Forwarded-* family + shared-secret gate + loopback bind + operator-must-overwrite prereq (oauth2-proxy CVE class). Majors folded in: alg pinning refuse-start on symmetric+JWKS; TLS-at-edge ack for non-loopback; per-verifier audience dropped (single resource); unknown-strategy/empty-composite fail-fast; introspection fail-closed + no-cache-in-v1 (instant revocation); no-log redaction; custom verifier as trusted-code boundary (importlib, no cwd on sys.path). Also peer-confirmed: DNS-rebinding "421" footgun handled (construct host/port/transport_security at FastMCP() time); behind-proxy discovery URLs from auth_resource_server_url; no token passthrough.

PLAN-REVIEW OUTCOME: epic + S1..S6 all reached PASS + signed (verify-signature certified). S7 authored but its review NOT yet run. Notable remediations discovered by the gate (real defects caught pre-code): flat-config incompatibility with nested [mcp.auth]; pyproject `mcp>=1.9` in TWO extras; gen_mcp_reference.py hardcoded tuple + gen_env_registry.py AST-scan miss; cache TTL formula confounding duration vs epoch (→ dropped caching); RFC 7662 aud string-OR-array + scope space-delimited + optional-field fallbacks; TOML-key vs REBAR_MCP_* env-var-name conflation; missing _config_schema.py file-impact.

OPERATIONAL NOTES FOR NEXT AGENT: (a) `rebar review-plan --output json` frequently returned EMPTY stdout to file this session (lost console output, likely rate-limit/flaky after ~45 invocations) — the SIGNED ATTESTATION on the ticket is the source of truth; check `rebar verify-signature <id> --kind plan-review`, NOT the captured JSON. (b) Run review-plan in BACKGROUND with NO hard timeout (it's long-running; a hard timeout or the 10-min foreground cap kills it) — per user guidance. (c) `setsid` is unavailable on macOS. (d) Do NOT run 3 reviews concurrently (killed with exit 144). (e) Ticket 2141 (audit UI) is ORTHOGONAL — no coupling; only shared concern is default port :8000 (S1 http_port is configurable).

NEXT ACTIONS: run `rebar review-plan fixed-jockeyish-swan` (S7); remediate any BLOCK; once all 8 signed, the epic is ready to claim + implement (per rebar-implement / TDD held-out flow, in a worktree off origin/main, landing each child as a stacked Gerrit change).
